user.name: Vincent Boucher · AGI.eth → ASI.eth
user.description: President, @Quebec_AI & @Montreal_AI. AGI.eth → ASI.eth. Sovereign Intelligence. Institutional Command. Govern capability before capability governs.

fullText:
Most AI demos show what a model can say.

This one shows what an institution can verify.

Introducing GoalOS MASTERCLASS — Contract-Exact Autonomous Proof Mission: a standalone, offline demonstration of a complete proof-to-settlement lifecycle, run autonomously as a deterministic smart-contract simulation.

One mission moves through:

Objective → Mission Contract → Escrow → Agent Assignment → ProofBundle → Independent Validation → Settlement → Chronicle → Governed Memory → Mission 2 Canary

Under the hood:

• 46 ABI-encoded calls
• Exact function selectors, calldata, events and state transitions
• Validator quorum—with dissent preserved
• Bonds, slashing and wei-level settlement reconciliation
• Authority gates, rollback conditions and a dedicated Revert Lab

No wallet. No RPC. No asset movement. No black box.

Every step answers:

What function ran?
Why was it permitted?
What changed?
What remains blocked?

For executives, builders and institutions exploring autonomous AI, verifiable machine work, smart-contract coordination and governed recursive improvement, this is the boardroom demonstration.

AI creates output. GoalOS creates proof.

Private executive walkthroughs are now open.

Demo: https://t.co/X9zGQa7jYr

#GoalOS #EnterpriseAI #AIGovernance #MontrealAI #QuebecAI

user.name: AIGCLINK
user.description: 致力于让每个想拥抱AI的人都能找到适合自己的AI产品，助力企业定制AIGC应用

fullText:
有人把一整个营销团队——CRO、文案、SEO、增长、定价、GTM、策略——打包成了 47 个 AI skill，装上之后，你的 Claude Code 就能当半个 CMO。

所有 skill 的地基是 product-marketing：任何其他 skill 动手前，都先读它，拿到"你的产品、受众、定位"。skill 间互相引用：copywriting ↔ cro ↔ ab-testing、customer-research → copywriting / cro / competitors，等于把一个真实营销团队的"共享上下文 + 分工协作SOP复刻进了 agent。同时，每个 skill 又都带 references/（方法库）和 evals/（真的做了评测）——是生产级的，不是攒一堆提示词。

几个特别的skill：

1、marketing-council：模拟一个"顾问董事会"，给你多个专家视角；
2、marketing-loops：让 agent 按周期自动跑的循环营销工作流（又是 loop / 自运行那套）；
3、ai-seo：专门优化"被 LLM 引用、出现在 AI 生成的答案里"——非常应景；
4、programmatic-seo：模板 + 数据批量出页。

"用这套 skill 当你自主 AI CMO"的 agent，装上这套skill，就能替代外包一半的营销活。

结合上周还在谈的"设计品味能不能打包成 skill"（hallmark、emilkowalski），这周整个营销职能也被系统化装进了 agent，两个结合就能跑出小的MVP。

#MarketingSkills #ClaudeCode #AI营销 #AgentSkills #增长

user.name: Santiago
user.description: Computer scientist. I teach hard-core AI/ML Engineering at https://t.co/THCAAZcBMu. YouTube: https://t.co/pROi08OZYJ

fullText:
This model can generate coherent 1+ hour videos across multiple scenes without skipping a beat.

I read their paper so you don't have to. Here is how it works:

First, this is an open-weight model. You can find the GitHub repo and link to the model weights below.

Most video models generate their videos one frame at a time. They look at previous frames to decide what comes next.

Unfortunately, if there's a mistake in one frame, every subsequent frame will compound the error. It gets ugly really quickly.

@robbyant_brain's new world model, LingBot-World-Infinity, works differently. Here are the main highlights:

• The model practices using its own mistakes. Since training only shows the model clean footage, it learns to generate videos that look perfect but never learns to recover when errors show up. Instead, this model runs on its own output for long stretches, letting it drift into the same flawed states it hits in real use.

• Then, the model gets shown the way back. From each of those flawed states, a slower and more careful version of the model demonstrates what a clean continuation looks like. The fast model adjusts its frames to match. It practices the recovery over and over, always starting from its own mess.

• The result is a model that steers itself back to center. Wherever it drifts, it knows the way back, because that recovery is exactly what it trained on. This is what lets it hold together for an hour instead of melting after a minute.

Alongside the 14B primary model, the paper describes a 1.3B variant designed to run on a single consumer GPU.

See links below:

user.name: Vincent Boucher · AGI.eth → ASI.eth
user.description: President, @Quebec_AI & @Montreal_AI. AGI.eth → ASI.eth. Sovereign Intelligence. Institutional Command. Govern capability before capability governs.

fullText:
AI demos usually end at output.

GoalOS begins where they stop: authority.

Introducing the GoalOS Partner MASTERCLASS — Autonomous Protocol Theatre: Complete Standalone Edition.

A cinematic, interactive, self-contained, offline institution that autonomously demonstrates the complete proof-to-authority lifecycle:

Objective → Mission Contract → Proof Debt → AGI Jobs → ProofBundles → Evidence Docket → Validation → Chronicle → Validated Skill → Merkle commitment → Proof-gated settlement → Governed RSI → Mission 2 → Challenge and rollback.

This is not a prerecorded animation.

The Theatre simulates the reference protocol mechanics in full view: smart-contract ABI calls, calldata, events, role checks, signatures, Merkle proofs, bonds, settlement gates, state transitions, expected reverts, tamper detection, revocation, and recovery.

Every action is inspectable.
Every authority transition leaves evidence.
Every failure remains visible.

No wallet connection.
No live funds.
No fabricated Mainnet status.
No claim of external validation.

Just a rigorous, beautiful, autonomous demonstration of how proof-carrying intelligence can operate as an institution—not merely as an agent.

AI creates output. GoalOS creates proof.

Bring one consequential objective.
Watch the institution prove what may safely matter next.

Demo: https://t.co/ecuTX3bXR1

#GoalOS #AIGovernance #AGIJobs

user.name: Kirk Borne

fullText:
Math Refresher for Adults — The Perfect Solution for Mastering Essential Math Skills: https://t.co/8Ly8NGHWzW

There are lots of reasons why you might want to have this book, no matter what your math proficiency might currently be. See the list of reasons on the book cover. 

My reason:
I tutored students in math when I was in high school and college, I took lots of math courses (including honors math classes for math majors) in college, I learned high-level math (group theory, differential forms, tensor calculus) from Nobel laureates in theoretical physics classes in grad school at Caltech, I taught math in my computational & data science courses as a university professor, I guided students who feared math into doing and loving calculus (conquering their fears and converting some into math lovers), and I sat for countless hours with my own children through many years of math homework & exam prep in their school years. My latest target audience are my grandkids. A book like this keeps me grounded in these new opportunities to share the love and beauty of math with a new generation.

Math Lovers-Я-us!

user.name: Kirk Borne
user.description: Advisor to startups. Freelancer. @LeadershipData founder. Global Speaker. Top B2B influencer & social promoter of Data Science AI ML. PhD Astrophysics @Caltech

fullText:
💥Scikit-learn Cookbook — 80+ recipes for Machine Learning in Python with scikit-learn [3rd Edition]: https://t.co/Z32D8jOzxC v/ @PacktDataML

𝓒𝓸𝓷𝓽𝓮𝓷𝓽𝓼:
🔹Common Conventions & API Elements of Scikit-Learn
🔹Pre-Model Workflow and Data Preprocessing
🔹Dimensionality Reduction Techniques
🔹Build Models with Distance Metrics & Nearest Neighbors
🔹Linear Models and Regularization
🔹Advanced Logistic Regression and Extensions
🔹Support Vector Machines and Kernel Methods
🔹Tree-Based Algorithms and Ensemble Methods
🔹Text Processing and Multiclass Classification
🔹Clustering Techniques
🔹Novelty and Outlier Detection
🔹Cross-Validation and Model Evaluation Techniques
🔹Deploying Scikit-Learn Models in Production

My Review (on Amazon):
I love this book, not only for its amazing content, but also for its brilliant adherence to its "Cookbook" label. The table of contents looks repetitive, but that's the magic of it. Find the algorithm, method, or application that you want "to cook up," and the presentation follows a uniform format from chapter to chapter. This is an extraordinarily practical, useful, and reader-friendly style, perfect for the target audience: anyone who needs to use (or is discovering how to use) specific Scikit-learn tools for their Python-based machine learning tasks. Consequently, this is great not only for individual use but also for training workshops and other educational settings. Python code in a cookbook context has never looked so good. IMHO.

user.name: AIGCLINK
user.description: 致力于让每个想拥抱AI的人都能找到适合自己的AI产品，助力企业定制AIGC应用

fullText:
这些天很火的AI剪辑师来了：ChatCut替代剪辑里最机械的那几步——导素材、拉时间线、配音乐、转写加字幕、导出——现在能一句话交给 agent 去做了。

也可以在codex里安装ChatCut-Inc/agent-plugin插件， 把视频剪辑器 ChatCut 就可以连到了 Codex：用自然语言描述需求，agent 就能替你导入媒体、改时间线、做动态图形、生成素材、转写音频、加字幕、导出，甚至回头验证这些改动在编辑器里真的生效了。

ChatCut 把自己的编辑能力暴露成一个托管 MCP server（https://t.co/GcPV2O7J7S），插件本体就是一个 Codex 插件：plugin.json（让 Codex 认得它）+ .mcp.json（连那个托管端点）+ 一组 skills/（把常见剪辑任务封装成工作流，让 agent 不必每次从零推理"这刀怎么剪"）。认证走 Codex 自己的登录流，插件不碰你的密钥。

ChatCut的商业模式也值得学习，它本身是个saas服务，把自己的能力变成 agent 可调用的接口，把整个产品的能力通过插件，标准化地开放给外部 agent 来操作。

使用的时候，示例 prompt很直白，对于剪辑的人来说解放双手："动嘴不动手"，对我这种懒人来说确实解决了问题，比如：

"把这段视频导进我的 ChatCut 项目"
"加一个简单的动态图形叠层"
"生成一段配音和背景音乐"
"转写这段并加上字幕"
"导出当前项目"

剪辑器的交互，正在从"你用鼠标在时间线上拖"变成"你用一句话让 agent 去拖"——ChatCut 把这条路，直接铺成了一个官方 MCP 插件。

#ChatCut #AI剪辑 #MCP #Codex #AI视频

fullText:
TRANSFER LEARNING book [390 pages]: https://t.co/mXv4YBGUjU

Amazon Summary:
"Transfer learning deals with how systems can quickly adapt themselves to new situations, tasks and environments. It gives machine learning systems the ability to leverage auxiliary data and models to help solve target problems when there is only a small amount of data available. This makes such systems more reliable and robust, keeping the machine learning model faced with unforeseeable changes from deviating too much from expected performance. At an enterprise level, transfer learning allows knowledge to be reused so experience gained once can be repeatedly applied to the real world."

user.name: elvis
user.description: Building self-improving AI @dair_ai • Prev: Meta AI | PhD • Learn about AI Agents for FREE here: https://t.co/P5SA9u54xO

fullText:
Another big reason to use combination of frontier models.

Chain-of-thought monitoring is treated as a reliable safety layer for agents. This DeepMind-affiliated study shows the layer can be argued out of doing its job.

Giving the monitor access to the agent reasoning trace raised approval of harmful actions by 9.5 percent on average, because the scratchpad becomes an extra channel for persuasion.

The fix was model diversity.

Pairing a Claude 3.7 Sonnet monitor with a GPT-4.1 fact-checker from a different family cut policy-violating approvals by up to 45 percent, versus only 6 percent when one model played both roles.

If your oversight rests on one model reading another model reasoning, an adversarial agent can talk its way past it. Cross-family fact-checking is the cheaper robustness lever here.

Paper: https://t.co/6qfuAEXqG6

Learn to build effective AI agents in our academy: https://t.co/1e8RZKs4uX
